title: senecillis dux
parts: Senecillis dux has many medicinal parts, such as roots, leaves, and flowers, which are utilized in traditional medicine. The roots of Senecillis dux contain constituents like senecionine, senkirkine, and senecionine N-oxide, which are known for their hepatoprotective properties. The leaves of the plant are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, including senecionides and sesquiterpene lactones A and B, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Additionally, the flowers of Senecillis dux contain flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Senecillis dux can have side effects if used improperly, such as liver damage, nausea, and vomiting, due to the presence of toxic compounds like pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Prolonged consumption of the plant can lead to scarring of the liver and potentially even cirrhosis. In severe cases, improper use of senecillis dux can cause respiratory failure and even death. It's essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
